evidence_quotes.quote: publishers cannot just kill off a game that you own, meaning you cannot play it at all.

paragraphs.text: Hello! I saw this initiative going on and thought I'd give a suggestion on what it could possibly include that would please a lot of people, including me, and can really only be a positive. If you have heard of the Stop Killing Games movement, it would basically be on that note, meaning that it would be cool if this initiative included protections for game preservation, in the way that publishers cannot just kill off a game that you own, meaning you cannot play it at all. There are a lot of examples of games being pulled off the shelves or functionally killed so you cannot play them at all anymore, meaning the game you bought and owned is just.. gone. This sucks, a lot, when it happens. So having more legislations on making sure publishers cannot kill off games that you own without recourse would be a win for the consumer and also means games can be preserved for future generations to also enjoy, instead of being lost to time forever. I hope you consider adding something like this to the initiative, if you would like to know more about what sort of ideas Stop Killing Games has for such preservation, they do have their own website, and more folk who can put such things into better words than I ever could. Thank you for reading this!
